3	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'

6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
9	import { resolvePrismaClient } from './resolvePrismaClient'
10	
11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}

import path from 'node:path'

import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { BuiltInProvider, parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'

import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
import { resolvePrismaClient } from './resolvePrismaClient'

type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
  runtimePath?: string
}

export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs

  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
  #runtimePath?: string
  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined

  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
  }

  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'

    return {
      defaultOutput,
      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
      version: clientVersion,
      requiresEngines: [],
      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
    }
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)

    await generateClient({
      datamodel: options.datamodel,
      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
      datasources: options.datasources,
      outputDir,
      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
      dmmf: options.dmmf,
      generator: options.generator,
      engineVersion: options.version,
      clientVersion,
      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
      typedSql: options.typedSql,
      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
    })
  }

  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
    }

    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
  }

  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
    if (this.#runtimePath) {
      return this.#runtimePath
    }

    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
    return this.#runtimePath
  }
}

function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
  if (value === undefined) {
    return 'fast'
  }
  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
    return value
  }
  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
}

### 9. **Generator Handler Protocol - `packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`**

```typescript
import readline from 'node:readline'

import type { GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'

import * as JsonRpc from './json-rpc'

export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void {
  const stdinInterface = readline.createInterface({
    input: process.stdin,
    crlfDelay: Infinity,
  })

  stdinInterface.on('line', async (line: string) => {
    const json = JSON.parse(line)

    if (json.method === 'generate' && json.params) {
      try {
        const result = await handler.onGenerate(json.params)
        respond({
          jsonrpc: '2.0',
          result: result,
          id: json.id,
        })
      } catch (_e) {
        const e = _e as Error
        respond({
          jsonrpc: '2.0',
          error: {
            code: -32000,
            message: e.message,
            data: {
              stack: e.stack,
            },
          },
          id: json.id,
        })
      }
    }

    if (json.method === 'getManifest') {
      if (handler.onManifest) {
        try {
          const manifest = await handler.onManifest(json.params)
          respond({
            jsonrpc: '2.0',
            result: {
              manifest,
            },
            id: json.id,
          })
        } catch (_e) {
          const e = _e as Error
          respond({
            jsonrpc: '2.0',
            error: {
              code: -32000,
              message: e.message,
              data: {
                stack: e.stack,
              },
            },
            id: json.id,
          })
        }
      } else {
        respond({
          jsonrpc: '2.0',
          result: {
            manifest: null,
          },
          id: json.id,
        })
      }
    }
  })

  process.stdin.resume()
}

function respond(response: JsonRpc.Response): void {
  process.stderr.write(JSON.stringify(response) + '\n')
}
```

## Key Takeaways for Creating a New Generator Package:

1. **Structure**: Copy the structure from `client-generator-js` or `client-generator-ts` with:
   - `helpers/build.ts` - Entry point that calls `build(adapterConfig)`
   - `src/` - Your source code
   - `tests/` - Test files
   - `tsconfig.json` - Extends root tsconfig
   - `tsconfig.build.json` - Extends tsconfig.build.regular.json with `outDir: dist` and `rootDir: src`

2. **Package.json Pattern**:
   - Use `"main": "dist/index.js"` and `"module": "dist/index.mjs"` for dual ESM/CJS
   - Add `exports` field for modern package exports
   - Include version 0.0.0 and workspace dependencies
   - Scripts: `dev` (DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts), `build` (tsx helpers/build.ts), `prepublishOnly`

3. **Generator Implementation**:
   - Implement the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`
   - Implement `getManifest()` returning `GeneratorManifest`
   - Implement `generate()` method that handles `GeneratorOptions`
   - Return a unique `name` from `BuiltInProvider` or string

4. **Generator Protocol**:
   - Uses JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout
   - Methods: `generate` and `getManifest`
   - Responses sent to stderr as JSON lines
   - Use `generatorHandler()` from `@prisma/generator-helper` for handling

5. **Build System**:
   - Uses esbuild with bundling
   - Generates CJS (.js) and ESM (.mjs) outputs simultaneously
   - Automatically emits TypeScript definitions
   - Externals are determined from package.json dependencies

### 6. **Generator Implementation - `packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`**

import path from 'node:path'

import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { BuiltInProvider, parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'

import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
import { resolvePrismaClient } from './resolvePrismaClient'

type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
  runtimePath?: string
}

export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs

  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
  #runtimePath?: string
  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined

  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
  }

  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'

    return {
      defaultOutput,
      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
      version: clientVersion,
      requiresEngines: [],
      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
    }
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)

    await generateClient({
      datamodel: options.datamodel,
      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
      datasources: options.datasources,
      outputDir,
      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
      dmmf: options.dmmf,
      generator: options.generator,
      engineVersion: options.version,
      clientVersion,
      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
      typedSql: options.typedSql,
      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
    })
  }

  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
    }

    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
  }

  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
    if (this.#runtimePath) {
      return this.#runtimePath
    }

    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
    return this.#runtimePath
  }
}

function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
  if (value === undefined) {
    return 'fast'
  }
  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
    return value
  }
  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
}

## Key Takeaways for Creating a New Generator Package:

1. **Structure**: Copy the structure from `client-generator-js` or `client-generator-ts` with:

   - `tsconfig.json` - Extends root tsconfig
   - `tsconfig.build.json` - Extends tsconfig.build.regular.json with `outDir: dist` and `rootDir: src`

2. **Package.json Pattern**:
   - Use `"main": "dist/index.js"` and `"module": "dist/index.mjs"` for dual ESM/CJS
   - Add `exports` field for modern package exports
   - Include version 0.0.0 and workspace dependencies
   - Scripts: `dev` (DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts), `build` (tsx helpers/build.ts), `prepublishOnly`

3. **Generator Implementation**:

   - Return a unique `name` from `BuiltInProvider` or string

4. **Generator Protocol**:
   - Uses JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout
   - Methods: `generate` and `getManifest`
   - Responses sent to stderr as JSON lines
   - Use `generatorHandler()` from `@prisma/generator-helper` for handling

5. **Build System**:
   - Uses esbuild with bundling
   - Generates CJS (.js) and ESM (.mjs) outputs simultaneously
   - Automatically emits TypeScript definitions
   - Externals are determined from package.json dependencies

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number

export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  type: IndexType
  isDefinedOnField: boolean
  name?: string
  dbName?: string
  algorithm?: string
  clustered?: boolean
  fields: IndexField[]
}>

export type IndexType = 'id' | 'normal' | 'unique' | 'fulltext'

export type IndexField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  sortOrder?: SortOrder
  length?: number
  operatorClass?: string
}>

export type SortOrder = 'asc' | 'desc'

export type Schema = ReadonlyDeep<{
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    // For now there are no `model` InputTypes
    model?: InputType[]
    prisma?: InputType[]
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]
    prisma: OutputType[]
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}>

export type Query = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: SchemaArg[]
  output: QueryOutput
}>

export type QueryOutput = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
}>

export type TypeRef<AllowedLocations extends FieldLocation> = {
  isList: boolean
  type: string
  location: AllowedLocations
  namespace?: FieldNamespace
}

export type InputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'>

export type SchemaArg = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  comment?: string
  isNullable: boolean
  isRequired: boolean
  inputTypes: InputTypeRef[]
  isParameterizable: boolean
  requiresOtherFields?: string[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
}>

export type OutputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: SchemaField[]
}>

export type SchemaField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isNullable?: boolean
  outputType: OutputTypeRef
  args: SchemaArg[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
  documentation?: string
}>

export type OutputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes'>

export type Deprecation = ReadonlyDeep<{
  sinceVersion: string
  reason: string
  plannedRemovalVersion?: string
}>

export type InputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  constraints: {
    maxNumFields: number | null
    minNumFields: number | null
    fields?: string[]
  }
  meta?: {
    source?: string
    grouping?: string
  }
  fields: SchemaArg[]

export type FieldRefType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  allowTypes: FieldRefAllowType[]
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefAllowType = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'enumTypes'>

  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}>

export enum ModelAction {
  findUnique = 'findUnique',
  findUniqueOrThrow = 'findUniqueOrThrow',
  findFirst = 'findFirst',
  findFirstOrThrow = 'findFirstOrThrow',
  findMany = 'findMany',
  create = 'create',
  createMany = 'createMany',
  createManyAndReturn = 'createManyAndReturn',
  update = 'update',
  updateMany = 'updateMany',
  updateManyAndReturn = 'updateManyAndReturn',
  upsert = 'upsert',
  delete = 'delete',
  deleteMany = 'deleteMany',
  groupBy = 'groupBy',
  count = 'count', // TODO: count does not actually exist in DMMF
  aggregate = 'aggregate',
  findRaw = 'findRaw',
  aggregateRaw = 'aggregateRaw',
}

Here's a complete summary of everything that was implemented:

---

## `@prisma/client-generator-rest` — New Generator Package

### Files created

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `packages/client-generator-rest/package.json` | Package manifest with `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/internals` as workspace deps |
| `packages/client-generator-rest/tsconfig.json` | Extends root tsconfig |
| `packages/client-generator-rest/tsconfig.build.json` | Build tsconfig targeting `dist/` |
| `packages/client-generator-rest/helpers/build.ts` | Delegates to `adapterConfig` (CJS + ESM dual build) |
| `src/scalar-type-map.ts` | Maps Prisma scalar names → TypeScript types (`String→string`, `Int/Float/Decimal→number`, `DateTime→Date`, etc.) |
| `src/model-codegen.ts` | Generates all interfaces + factory for one model |
| `src/generate-rest-client.ts` | Orchestrates generation and writes `index.ts` to `outputDir` |
| `src/generator.ts` | `RestApiGenerator` class implementing the `Generator` interface |
| `src/index.ts` | Public re-exports |
| `src/__tests__/generate-rest-client.test.ts` | 30 Vitest tests (all passing) |

`tsconfig.build.bundle.json` was updated to add the `@prisma/client-generator-rest` path mapping.

### What gets generated

For every Prisma model the generator emits five TypeScript interfaces and a factory function:

```typescript
// Model shape (all fields)
export interface User { id: number; email: string; name: string | null }

// Write inputs — honours @id/@default, @updatedAt, nullability
export interface UserCreateInput { email: string; name?: string | null }
export interface UserUpdateInput { email?: string; name?: string | null }

// Filter inputs
export interface UserWhereInput   { id?: number; email?: string; … }
export interface UserWhereUniqueInput { id?: number; email?: string }  // @id + @unique only

// Typed CRUD client interface
export interface UserClient {
  findMany(where?: UserWhereInput): Promise<User[]>
  findUnique(where: UserWhereUniqueInput): Promise<User | null>
  create(data: UserCreateInput): Promise<User>
  update(where: UserWhereUniqueInput, data: UserUpdateInput): Promise<User>
  delete(where: UserWhereUniqueInput): Promise<User>
}
```

Enums become `as const` objects with a companion union type. The top-level factory wires everything together:

```typescript
const client = createPrismaRestApiClient({ baseUrl: 'https://api.example.com' })
const users = await client.user.findMany()
const post  = await client.post.create({ data: { title: 'Hello' } })
```

### Key design decisions
- **Relation fields** are skipped — REST representations are flat; joins belong to the API layer.
- **`@updatedAt` / auto-generated id fields** are excluded from `CreateInput`/`UpdateInput` — the server manages them.
- **Model name → URL path** uses kebab-case (`BlogPost → /blog-post`).
- **`httpRequest`** is a private helper in the generated file; consumers only see the typed client interfaces.
